config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Society’s interests matter a lot, but they should not automatically come before individual rights, because rights are what protect people from abuse by the majority or the state. In my view, a society is strongest when it balances the common good with clear limits on what can be taken away from each person. If individual rights are treated as secondary too easily, it becomes too simple to justify unfair treatment in the name of order or progress.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diutamakan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu justru menjadi batas penting terhadap mayoritas dan pemerintah, karena tanpa batas itu, “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Tentu saja masyarakat punya kepentingan nyata, seperti keamanan dan kesehatan publik, tetapi prioritas itu tidak boleh menghapus hak dasar orang secara sembarangan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menekan individu paling lemah, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kepentingan umum tanpa mengorbankan kebebasan dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically override individual rights, because in Indonesia we know how easily “for the public good” can be used to silence criticism or pressure minorities. At the same time, I also do not see rights as absolute in every situation, because public health, safety, and social harmony can require real limits. The key point is that those limits must be narrow, fair, and accountable, not a blank check for the state or the majority. If society becomes the excuse to erase individual dignity, then the society itself becomes unjust.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak klaim bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu, karena dari perspektif Amerika, hak individu justru ada untuk membatasi mayoritas dan negara. Saya paham argumen tentang kesehatan publik, keamanan, dan harmoni sosial, tetapi menurut saya itu hanya sah kalau pembatasannya sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan; kalau tidak, “demi masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi alasan untuk menekan minoritas atau membungkam kritik. Saya juga tidak setuju jika hak individu diperlakukan seolah-olah selalu bisa dinegosiasikan demi hasil kolektif yang lebih nyaman. Masyarakat yang benar-benar kuat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan orang per orang, melainkan yang mampu menjaga kepentingan umum tanpa menghapus kebebasan dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should generally take priority over the individual, because in Indonesia the phrase “for the common good” has too often been used to excuse coercion, silence criticism, or pressure minorities. That said, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every case, since public health, safety, and order can justify limited restrictions when they are necessary and tightly controlled. What I disagree with is turning society into the default trump card, because once that happens, the weakest people are usually the first to lose. A fair society protects the common good by respecting rights first, not by treating rights as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus menjadi prioritas utama atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat hak individu sebagai pagar yang justru mencegah mayoritas dan pemerintah bertindak semena-mena, dan sejarah kami penuh pelajaran bahwa alasan “demi masyarakat” bisa dipakai untuk membungkam kritik atau menekan kelompok rentan. Memang benar ada situasi seperti kesehatan publik dan keamanan yang memerlukan pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan dapat diawasi, bukan dijadikan alasan umum untuk mengorbankan kebebasan dasar. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat tidak menghapus hak individu demi kenyamanan kolektif, melainkan menjaga keduanya dengan memberi batas tegas pada kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
